items.title: What Iran says is in the Donald Trump deal and the item 'removed from the agenda'

children.content: Iran agreeing to a ceasefire deal with the United States tells us Tehran likely had something to gain from signing on. This is what Iran is promoting as the 14-point deal.

articles.title: The ultimate 'infidel' killed Khamenei. These women want revenge on Trump

children.content: As we journey into the heart of Iran, we meet the women who support the clerics and want revenge on the man who killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

image.altText: Women in traditional Islamic attire with their hands raised in mourning.

articles.title: Farmer fertiliser hope as Iran ceasefire promises to reopen supply

children.content: Farmers are hoping the US-Iran deal will help free up fertiliser supply and lower costs during the winter cropping season.

image.altText: A hand holds brilliant white fertiliser while a truck tips it off in the background

gallery.altText: An image of Donald Trump in front of a map of Iran.

thumbnail.altText: An image of Donald Trump in front of a map of Iran.

items.title: Trump intensifies pressure on Netanyahu over Lebanon attacks

children.content: The US president levels more criticism at Israel's prime minister and suggests asking Syria to take on Hezbollah "if Israel can't do the job without killing everyone else".

image.altText: Donald Trump stands in front of a 'G7 France' sign.

items.title: Why the market thinks Michele Bullock is bluffing on rate hikes

children.content: Michele Bullock made it abundantly clear that the RBA was not ruling out further interest rate rises, but the market appears to be unswayed by the tough talk.

image.altText: A dark-haired woman wearing glasses and a blazer leans on a lectern in front of an RBA-branded backdrop.

items.title: Kyle Sandilands's contract lawsuit reaches multi-million-dollar settlement

children.content: Veteran Sydney broadcaster Kyle Sandilands and ARN Media have reached a settlement to end a $100 million contract dispute which had been playing out in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Sandilands wearing a navy suit and sunglasses

items.title: What a trillion dollars costs democracy

children.content: Democracy cannot survive trillionaires. We need to start taxing billionaires properly.

image.altText: A man in a suit jacket gestures while speaking.

images.altText: Nicolas Jackson holds his hands to his face

children.content: Day six at the World Cup features a number of superstar strikers, beginning with a blockbuster between Kylian Mbappe's France and Senegal at New York New Jersey Stadium. Follow live.

items.title: Mbappe scores stunner after Aussie ref's 'nonsensical' VAR call
Tim Wilson holds undisclosed shares in climate advisory firm through family trust

children.content: The shadow treasurer's investment trust holds 15,000 shares in a small company called Gondwana Carbon, which helps developers to acquire land, develop carbon abatement projects, earn carbon credits and then trade them.

image.altText: Tim Wilson at National Press Club

items.title: Government to extend pay subsidy to avoid childcare worker walk off

children.content: Early educators had threatened to walk off the job to keep a federally funded 15 per cent pay subsidy due to end in November.

image.altText: Unidentified child's hands playing with building bricks.

items.title: 'I didn't have a life': Pensioner moves to Thailand for cheaper living

children.content: Peter Stevenson says moving to Thailand has allowed him to live more comfortably on his Australian Age Pension after struggling to cover rent and essentials in regional Victoria.

gallery.altText: A selfie of a smiling man in silver-rimmed glasses and a grey tee taken from inside a restauraunt with red lanterns.

thumbnail.altText: A selfie of a smiling man in silver-rimmed glasses and a grey tee taken from inside a restauraunt with red lanterns.

items.title: Joyce cautions Hanson ahead of National Press Club address

children.content: Barnaby Joyce has cautioned his leader Pauline Hanson against falling for "gotcha questions" during her address to the National Press Club today. Follow live.

image.altText: Barnaby Joyce gestures while speaking at press conference.

items.title: Gout runs a stunning third as Lyles breaks world record

children.content: Rising Australian superstar Gout Gout pushes American champion Noah Lyles all the way in their first race against each other.

image.altText: Two men hug after a running race.

items.title: 'It's all too hard': Fuel shock pushes regional businesses to the brink

children.content: Small-town coffee shops and long-established businesses are at risk of closing despite a ceasefire in the Middle East easing the global oil price. Regional businesses say fuel relief measures need to be extended because the damage done from the fuel shock is far from over.

gallery.altText: A caravan park with a closed sign at its entrance.
A woman in a cafe stands behind the counter near her coffee machine.

thumbnail.altText: A caravan park with a closed sign at its entrance.
A dairy farmer stands with arms crossed with cows behind him in pens
A woman in a cafe stands behind the counter near her coffee machine.

items.title: El Niño declared by BOM and it could become the strongest on record

children.content: El Niño is a state that can linger for up to 12 months and disrupt weather patterns across the globe, with Australia especially prone.

image.altText: A graphic of the Earth with the majority of areas highlighted in yellow and orange indicating warmer weather around Aus.

items.title: Dealing with NDIS leading to anxiety and suicidal ideation among participants

children.content: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

image.altText: Andrew Bretherton dressed in pants and dark jumper and jacket sits in a wheelchair.

items.title: Lorne residents' invention solution to town's marauding cockatoos

children.content: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the town of Lorne.

gallery.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".
Rubbish is scattered around four open blue and red wheelie bins where cockatoos sit in a car park on a cloudy day.
About 10 white cockatoos sit on wooden table tops and seats outside a cafe.

thumbnail.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".
Rubbish is scattered around four open blue and red wheelie bins where cockatoos sit in a car park on a cloudy day.

items.title: Looking for something to play? Here are the best games of 2026 so far

children.content: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

image.altText: Three people shaped characters stand with a pokemon trubbish

items.title: Ann Patchett on why stalkers want her to read their novels

children.content: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

image.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

items.title: Jacinta Allan refuses to admit cost of CFMEU corruption to taxpayers

children.content: Embattled Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan has refused to answer repeated questions over how much CFMEU corruption has cost taxpayers in the state, a scandal that has put the government and her leadership under threat.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses holds a document while speaking to reporters in a conference room.

items.title: Police save forager from potentially lethal death cap mushroom mix-up

children.content: Police say a Perth man allegedly mistook the mushrooms he illegally foraged for their psychedelic counterparts.

image.altText: Supplied photo of a poisonous mushroom seized by WA Police.

items.title: State of Origin quiz: Tackle our More or Less challenge ahead of Game II

children.content: Today's ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to go deep into AFL territory and come away with the win. Mouthguards in, let's go.

articles.title: 'Unfair on him': Origin coaches back ref after gambling history revealed

children.content: Billy Slater and Laurie Daley come to the defence of referee Ashley Klein after his gambling debt history was revealed earlier in the week.

image.altText: An referee points at a player to leave the field.

articles.title: What time is State of Origin Game II tonight?

children.content: NSW and Queensland meet at the famous MCG in State of Origin Game II. Here is what you need to know ahead of tonight's match.

image.altText: Reuben Cotter of the Maroons is tackled by Isaah Yeo and Hudson Young of the Blues

gallery.altText: New South Wales and Queensland players fight

items.title: Russian artist who satirised Putin shot dead in Poland

children.content: Two Belarusian nationals are arrested in connection with the killing, authorities say.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat at a demonstration on a street.

items.title: Fatal US B-52 bomber crash happened at more than 1,500m per minute

children.content: A B-52 bomber aircraft that crashed at a US Air Force base in California, killing eight people aboard, made a sharp right and then nearly completed a 180-degree turn before plunging to the ground.

image.altText: A long black burn mark on an air base runway smouldering with black smoke near parked vehicles.

items.title: 'Life-changing' school for autistic children saved from 'chopping block'

children.content: The private Sycamore School in Alexandra Hills is so sought after, some parents drive almost 50 kilometres one-way to drop off their kids, but it was "on the chopping block".

avatar.alt: A journalist holding a microphone and smiling.

items.title: UK investigates reports Russian ship fired at yacht in English Channel

children.content: Russia's defence ministry said it fired the warning shots to divert the yacht that approached on a dangerous course in the English Channel.

image.altText: A navy frigate passing through the Bosphorus, with Istanbul in the background.

items.title: FBI says plot to attack White House UFC event with drones thwarted

children.content: US media says five people are in custody in connection with the plot, that involved flying explosive-laden drones towards buildings near last weekend's White House UFC match.

image.altText: A canopy over a UFC ring in the dark sky with the White House in the background

items.title: Time for Russia to make a deal to end Ukraine war, Trump says

children.content: Donald Trump says Moscow should "make a deal" to end the war with Ukraine as G7 leaders have agreed to intensify pressure on Russia.

image.altText: Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Emmanuel Macron smiling.

items.title: Roadside reptile mystery continues as remains confirmed as saltwater croc

children.content: Reptile remains found dumped on the side of an Adelaide roadside have been confirmed as belonging to a saltwater crocodile, but there is still no explanation for how they came to be there.

image.altText: crocodile with its mouth open on a muddy river bank.

items.title: After the first attempt failed, outback town gives renewables another go

children.content: This outback town is giving renewable energy another shot as a new solar farm and battery system hooks up to the grid, two decades after a solar array project failed to live up to the hype.

gallery.altText: Hundreds of solar panels in a field.

thumbnail.altText: Hundreds of solar panels in a field.

items.title: How this $480 million NSW renewable energy scheme could affect your home

children.content: Interest-free loans and cash subsidies are being offered by the New South Wales government to encourage the uptake of renewable energy measures like solar panels and household batteries.

image.altText: Two men in orange hi-vis clothing stand on a roof holding a large solar panel ready to attach it.

items.title: Charge entry fees to Kunanyi/Mt Wellington, park management told

children.content: A tourism consultancy firm has recommended that visitors to Hobart's Kunanyi/Mt Wellington be charged for access — likely to result in a "deliberate and positive outcome" of fewer people going.

image.altText: Mt Wellington

items.title: Two men arrested by Dezi Freeman investigators

children.content: Police executed seven warrants at properties across Victoria and New South Wales this morning.

image.altText: A screen grab of a man looking seriously at a the camera.

items.title: Birth doula says lack of consent delayed Triple Zero call for dying mother

children.content: A court probing the death of Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth has heard that emergency care was delayed for several minutes because her "birth keeper" would only call an ambulance if she consented.

image.altText: Stacey Warnecke smiles at the camera.

items.title: Ex-Hawke minister-turned Catholic priest farewelled in Hobart

children.content: Michael Tate — an academic, politician, diplomat and Catholic priest — has been remembered as a generous man whose life was one of "warmth and service to others".

gallery.altText: Michael Tate wears a black suit, black shirt and priest's collar and stands outside the University of Tasmania law faculty.

thumbnail.altText: Michael Tate wears a black suit, black shirt and priest's collar and stands outside the University of Tasmania law faculty.

items.title: Craft retailer Lincraft to close rest of its stores in Australia and NZ

children.content: Retailer Lincraft will close the remainder of its shopfronts after more than 80 years of providing fabric, materials and homewares to Aussie crafters.

image.altText: A large white-on-blue 'Lincraft' sign hangs over a storefront, with the tag line 'where ideas come to life'.

items.title: 38yo man jailed for life over 'highly brutal murder' of 22yo wife in NT

children.content: Braden Jentian, 38, has been sentenced to life behind bars for the "protracted and highly brutal murder" of his 22-year-old female partner, whose body was found in a vacant block in Katherine in 2024.

image.altText: A white, modernist, flat-fronted building with "supreme court" in capital letters above a wide entrance.
